L. 116–21, § 1(c)133 Stat. 903June 12, 2019“(1).—DefinitionsIn this subsection, the terms ‘contractor’, ‘covered individual’, and ‘food services contract’ have the meanings given those terms in ().section 1(a) of Public Law 110–2792 U.S.C. 2051(a)“(2).—ApplicabilityThe amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to—“(A)a covered individual who separates from service as an employee of a contractor performing services under the food services contract before, on, or after the date of enactment of this Act []; andJune 12, 2019“(B)each payment to a covered individual under chapter 83 or 84 of title 5, United States Code, made on or after the effective date of the regulations promulgated under subsection (b) [set out as a note below].”

children.heading: Contractor
children.label: 1 Contractor
children.text: The term “contractor” means the private business concern that enters into a food services contract with the Architect of the Capitol.

children.label: i
children.text: a permanent, full-time or part-time employee;

children.label: ii
children.text: a temporary, full-time or part-time employee; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an employee in a position described under section 2048 of this title;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: becomes an employee of the contractor under a food services contract on the transfer date; and

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to benefits under subsection (c)(2) or (3), files an election before the transfer date with the Office of Human Resources of the Architect of the Capitol to have 1 or more benefits continued in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Food services contract
children.label: 3 Food services contract
children.text: The term “food services contract” means a contract under which food services operations of the Senate Restaurants are transferred to, and performed by, a private business concern.

children.heading: Transfer date
children.label: 4 Transfer date
children.text: The term “transfer date” means the date on which a contractor begins the performance of food services operations under a food services contract.

children.heading: Retirement coverage
children.label: A Retirement coverage
children.text: Not later than the day before the transfer date, an individual described under subsection (a)(2)(A) and (B) may file an election with the Office of Human Resources of the Architect of the Capitol to continue coverage under the retirement system under which that individual is covered on that day.

children.heading: Life and health insurance coverage
children.label: B Life and health insurance coverage
children.text: If the individual files an election under subparagraph (A) to continue retirement coverage, the individual may also file an election with the Office of Human Resources of the Architect of the Capitol to continue coverage of any other benefit under subsection (c)(2) or (3) for which that individual is covered on that day. Any election under this subparagraph shall be filed not later than the day before the transfer date.

children.heading: Notification to the Office of Personnel Management
children.label: 2 Notification to the Office of Personnel Management
children.text: The Office of Human Resources of the Architect of the Capitol shall provide timely notification to the Office of Personnel Management of any election filed under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Pay
children.label: 1 Pay
children.text: The rate of basic pay of a covered individual as an employee of a contractor, or successor contractor, during a period of continuous service may not be reduced to a rate less than the rate of basic pay paid to that individual as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol on the day before the transfer date, except for cause.

children.heading: Treatment of service
children.label: i Treatment of service
children.text: For purposes of chapters 83, 84, and 87 of title 5, any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual as an employee of a contractor, or successor contractor, shall be deemed to be a period of service as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Treatment of pay
children.label: ii Treatment of pay
children.text: For purposes of chapter 87 of title 5, the rate of basic pay of a covered individual during the period described under clause (i) shall be deemed to be the rate of basic pay of that individual as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol on the date on which the Architect of the Capitol enters into the food services contract.

children.heading: Treatment as Civil Service Retirement Offset employees
children.label: B Treatment as Civil Service Retirement Offset employees
I
children.text: employment of an individual described under ; andsection 8402(b)(2) of title 5

children.label: II
children.text: Federal service as defined under ; andsection 8349(c) of title 5

children.label: ii
children.text: the basic pay described under subparagraph (A)(ii) for employment described under subparagraph (A)(i) shall be deemed to be Federal wages as defined under .section 8334(k)(2)(C)(i) of title 5

children.heading: Health insurance benefits
children.label: 3 Health insurance benefits
children.text: For purposes of chapters 89, 89A, and 89B of title 5, any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual as an employee of a contractor, or successor contractor, shall be deemed to be a period of service as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Credit of leave
children.label: A Credit of leave
children.text: Subject to , annual and sick leave balances of any covered individual shall be credited to the leave accounts of that individual as an employee of the contractor, or any successor contractor. A food services contract may include provisions similar to regulations prescribed under to implement this subparagraph.section 6304 of title 5section 6308 of title 5

children.heading: Accrual rate
children.label: B Accrual rate
children.text: During any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual as an employee of a contractor, or successor contractor, that individual shall continue to accrue annual and sick leave at rates not less than the rates applicable to that individual on the day before the transfer date.

children.heading: Transit subsidy
children.label: 5 Transit subsidy
children.text: For purposes of any benefit under , any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual as an employee of a contractor, or successor contractor, shall be deemed to be a period of service as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol.section 7905 of title 5

children.heading: Employee pay; Government contributions; transit subsidy payments; and other benefits
children.label: 6 Employee pay; Government contributions; transit subsidy payments; and other benefits
i
children.text: the pay of a covered individual as an employee of a contractor, or successor contractor, during a period of continuous service;

children.label: ii
children.text: Government contributions for the benefits of a covered individual under paragraph (2) or (3);

children.label: iii
children.text: any transit subsidy for a covered individual under paragraph (5); and

children.label: iv
children.text: any payment for any other benefit for a covered individual in accordance with a food services contract.

children.heading: Reimbursements and payments by Architect of the Capitol
children.label: B Reimbursements and payments by Architect of the Capitol
i
children.text: reimburse a contractor, or any successor contractor, for that portion of any payment under subparagraph (A) which the Architect of the Capitol agreed to pay under a food services contract; and

children.label: ii
children.text: pay a contractor, or any successor contractor, for any administrative fee (or portion of an administrative fee) which the Architect of the Capitol agreed to pay under a food services contract.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: After consultation with the Architect of the Capitol, the Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall prescribe regulations to provide for the continuity of benefits under paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.label: I
children.text: include regulations relating to employee deductions and employee and employer contributions and deposits in the Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, the Employees’ Life Insurance Fund, and the Employees Health Benefits Fund; and

children.label: II
children.text: provide for the Architect of the Capitol to perform employer administrative functions necessary to ensure administration of continued coverage of benefits under paragraphs (2) and (3), including receipt and transmission of the deductions, contributions, and deposits described under subclause (I), the collection and transmission of such information as necessary, and the performance of other administrative functions as may be required.

children.heading: Thrift Savings Plan benefits
children.label: B Thrift Savings Plan benefits
children.text: After consultation with the Architect of the Capitol, the Executive Director appointed by the Federal Retirement Thrift Investment Board under shall prescribe regulations to provide for the continuity of benefits under paragraph (2) of this subsection relating to subchapter III of chapter 84 of that title. Regulations under this subparagraph shall include regulations relating to employee deductions and employee and employer contributions and deposits in the Thrift Savings Fund.section 8474(a) of title 5

children.heading: Covered individuals not entitled to severance pay
children.label: d Covered individuals not entitled to severance pay
A
children.text: separation from service with the Architect of the Capitol and becoming an employee of a contractor under a food services contract; or

children.label: B
children.text: termination of employment with a contractor, or successor to a contractor.

children.heading: Covered individuals
children.label: i Covered individuals
children.text: Except as provided under clause (ii), a covered individual shall be entitled to severance pay under if during the 90-day period following the transfer date the employment of that individual with a contractor is terminated as provided under a food services contract.section 5595 of title 5

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to a covered individual who is terminated for cause.

children.label: i
children.text: any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual described under subparagraph (A) as an employee of a contractor shall be deemed to be a period of service as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any termination of employment of a covered individual described under subparagraph (A) with a contractor shall be treated as a separation from service with the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Submission of plan
children.label: 1 Submission of plan
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Architect of the Capitol shall submit a plan under to the applicable committees as provided under that section.July 17, 2008section 4505 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: offer a voluntary separation incentive payment to any employee described under subsection (a)(2)(A) of this section in accordance with ; andsection 4505 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: offer such a payment to any such employee who becomes a covered individual, if that individual accepts the offer during the 90-day period following the transfer date.

children.label: i
children.text: any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual as an employee of a contractor shall be deemed to be a period of service as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any termination of employment of a covered individual with a contractor shall be treated as a separation from service with the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Early retirement treatment for certain separated employees
children.label: f Early retirement treatment for certain separated employees
i
children.text: voluntarily separates from service on or after , but prior to the day before the transfer date; andJuly 17, 2008

children.label: I
children.text: has completed 25 years of service as defined under section 8331(12) or 8401(26) of title 5; or

children.label: II
children.text: has completed 20 years of such service and is at least 50 years of age; and

children.label: i
children.text: whose employment with a contractor is terminated as provided under a food services contract during the 90-day period following the transfer date; and

children.label: I
children.text: has completed 25 years of service as defined under section 8331(12) or 8401(26) of title 5; or

children.label: II
children.text: has completed 20 years of such service and is at least 50 years of age.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1)(B) shall not apply to a covered individual who is terminated for cause.

children.heading: Annuity
children.label: A Annuity
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of chapter 83 or 84 of title 5, an employee described under paragraph (1) is entitled to an annuity which shall be computed consistent with the provisions of law applicable to annuities under section 8336(d) or 8414(b) of title 5.

children.label: i
children.text: any period of continuous service performed by a covered individual described under paragraphs (1)(B) and (2) as an employee of a contractor shall be deemed to be a period of service as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any termination of employment of a covered individual described under paragraphs (1)(B) and (2) with a contractor shall be treated as a separation from service with the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Continuing application to certain acts and omissions
children.label: 3 Continuing application to certain acts and omissions
children.text: For purposes of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 ( et seq.) a covered individual shall be treated as an employee of the Architect of the Capitol with respect to any act or omission which occurred before the transfer date.2 U.S.C. 1301

children.heading: Senate Restaurants food services contract
children.label: 1 Senate Restaurants food services contract
children.text: Any commissions paid by a contractor under a food services contract shall be deposited in the miscellaneous items account within the contingent fund of the Senate.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Any funds deposited under paragraph (1) shall be available for expenditure in the same manner as funds appropriated into that account.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: i Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect on , and apply to the remainder of the fiscal year in which enacted and each fiscal year thereafter.July 17, 2008
